src/index.ts.summary: Public package entry point; re-exports the format/formatDialect API, expandPhrases, ConfigError, every dialect DialectOptions object, and the public types.
src/index.ts.outputs: named exports: functions, dialect objects, and types
src/index.ts.should: Barrel-export the library's public surface; stay in sync with the dialect list.
src/index.ts.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or tokenizing behavior of its own.

src/sqlFormatter.ts.summary: Top-level format() and formatDialect() entry functions; maps a language name to a DialectOptions, merges defaults, validates config, and runs Formatter.
src/sqlFormatter.ts.inputs: query string, FormatOptionsWithLanguage or FormatOptionsWithDialect config
src/sqlFormatter.ts.outputs: formatted SQL string; throws ConfigError/Error on bad input
src/sqlFormatter.ts.should: Resolve dialect by name (incl. tsql alias), apply defaultOptions, validate, delegate to Formatter.
src/sqlFormatter.ts.shouldNot: Tokenize, parse, or format directly; know dialect internals.

src/FormatOptions.ts.summary: Type-only module declaring the FormatOptions interface and the case/indent/newline string-literal option types.
src/FormatOptions.ts.should: Define the user-facing formatting option shape.
src/FormatOptions.ts.shouldNot: Contain runtime code or default values.

src/allDialects.ts.summary: Barrel that re-exports every dialect's DialectOptions object from its formatter module, used by sqlFormatter to look dialects up by name.
src/allDialects.ts.should: Aggregate all dialect definitions in one namespace.
src/allDialects.ts.shouldNot: Add logic or transform the dialects.

src/dialect.ts.summary: Defines DialectOptions/Dialect types and createDialect(): a cached factory that instantiates a Tokenizer and processes format options into lookup maps.
src/dialect.ts.should: Cache built dialects by options identity; precompute oneline-clause lookup records.
src/dialect.ts.shouldNot: Format or tokenize; hold user FormatOptions.

src/expandPhrases.ts.summary: Expands bracket/brace SQL phrase templates like 'CREATE [OR REPLACE] TABLE' into every concrete keyword-sequence string via a small recursive-descent parser.
src/expandPhrases.ts.outputs: flat array of expanded keyword strings
src/expandPhrases.ts.should: Parse [] optional / {} mandatory-alternation blocks and enumerate all combinations; throw on unbalanced parens.
src/expandPhrases.ts.shouldNot: Know about tokenizing or SQL semantics.

src/validateConfig.ts.summary: Defines ConfigError and validateConfig(), which rejects removed options, non-positive expressionWidth, and empty custom paramType regexes; warns on non-string params.
src/validateConfig.ts.outputs: same config if valid; throws ConfigError otherwise
src/validateConfig.ts.should: Enumerate and reject each invalid/removed option case.

src/utils.ts.summary: Small generic string/array helpers (dedupe, last, sortByLengthDesc, maxLength, equalizeWhitespace, isMultiline) plus the Optional type utility.
src/utils.ts.side: sortByLengthDesc sorts its argument in place

src/formatter/Formatter.ts.summary: Main Formatter class: parses a query into StatementNodes, formats each via ExpressionFormatter, joins them with blank lines, and appends semicolons.
src/formatter/Formatter.ts.should: Orchestrate parse->format per statement; honor linesBetweenQueries and newlineBeforeSemicolon.
src/formatter/Formatter.ts.shouldNot: Implement node-level formatting or tokenizing itself.

src/formatter/ExpressionFormatter.ts.summary: Core recursive AST formatter: walks each node type (clauses, parens, CASE, joins, operators, comments, keywords) emitting tokens and whitespace into a Layout, handling case-conversion and inline vs multiline.
src/formatter/ExpressionFormatter.ts.should: Render every AstNode type; apply keyword/identifier/dataType casing; try inline layout then fall back.
src/formatter/ExpressionFormatter.ts.shouldNot: Parse or tokenize; access the compiler/lexer directly.
src/formatter/ExpressionFormatter.ts.side: mutates shared Layout and Params positional index

src/formatter/Indentation.ts.summary: Indentation class tracking a stack of top-level and block-level indent markers to compute the current indent depth.
src/formatter/Indentation.ts.should: Push/pop indent types; discard nested top-levels when a block closes.
src/formatter/Indentation.ts.shouldNot: Emit output strings or know token types.

src/formatter/Layout.ts.summary: Defines the WS whitespace-modifier enum and Layout class that accumulates token strings + whitespace items and renders them to a final string, trimming redundant whitespace.
src/formatter/Layout.ts.should: Model spacing/newlines/indent as items and collapse whitespace on demand.
src/formatter/Layout.ts.shouldNot: Know AST node types or config options.

src/formatter/InlineLayout.ts.summary: InlineLayout subclass of Layout that measures line width and throws InlineLayoutError when a newline appears or expressionWidth is exceeded.
src/formatter/InlineLayout.ts.should: Track running length/trailing space; reject multiline or over-wide expressions.
src/formatter/InlineLayout.ts.shouldNot: Apply indentation or format node semantics.

src/formatter/Params.ts.summary: Params class resolving parameter placeholders to substitution values, supporting named-key and positional-index lookup.
src/formatter/Params.ts.outputs: substituted string (or original text when no params)
src/formatter/Params.ts.should: Return keyed value or next positional value; expose/restore positional index.

src/formatter/config.ts.summary: Config helper functions: indentString() computes one indent step from options and isTabularStyle() detects tabular indent modes.

src/formatter/tabularStyle.ts.summary: toTabularFormat() pads keyword text to a fixed 10-char column for tabular indent styles, and isTabularToken() flags which token types get tabular treatment.
src/formatter/tabularStyle.ts.should: Left/right-pad keywords in tabular mode; pass through in standard mode.
src/formatter/tabularStyle.ts.shouldNot: Manage indentation levels or emit whitespace.

src/lexer/Tokenizer.ts.summary: Tokenizer class that builds an ordered list of regex TokenRules from a dialect's TokenizerOptions (comments, keywords, params, operators, identifiers) and tokenizes input via TokenizerEngine.
src/lexer/Tokenizer.ts.should: Order rules for correct precedence; canonicalize keywords to uppercase; support per-call param overrides.
src/lexer/Tokenizer.ts.side: none (caches rule arrays on the instance)

src/lexer/TokenizerEngine.ts.summary: TokenizerEngine plus TokenRule/RegExpLike interfaces: scans an input string left-to-right, skipping whitespace and matching the first applicable rule to emit Tokens; throws a positioned parse error on no match.
src/lexer/TokenizerEngine.ts.should: Advance by matched length, attach precedingWhitespace, report line/col on failure.
src/lexer/TokenizerEngine.ts.shouldNot: Build rules or know dialect specifics.
src/lexer/TokenizerEngine.ts.side: mutates internal index/input during a run

src/lexer/TokenizerOptions.ts.summary: Type-only module declaring TokenizerOptions and its supporting types (IdentChars, QuoteType, VariableType, ParamTypes, CustomParameter) that describe a dialect's lexical config.
src/lexer/TokenizerOptions.ts.should: Define the shape each dialect fills in for tokenizing.

src/lexer/disambiguateTokens.ts.summary: Post-tokenize pass that resolves ambiguities: demotes keywords next to a dot or non-followed-by-paren function names to identifiers, and promotes data types before ( or [ to parameterized/array variants.
src/lexer/disambiguateTokens.ts.should: Retype tokens based on neighboring non-comment tokens.
src/lexer/disambiguateTokens.ts.shouldNot: Emit output or depend on formatting config.

src/lexer/lineColFromIndex.ts.summary: lineColFromIndex() converts a character index in source text into 1-based line and column numbers.
src/lexer/lineColFromIndex.ts.should: Count newlines up to the index.

src/lexer/regexFactory.ts.summary: Builder functions that assemble sticky RegExps for each lexical category (comments, strings, identifiers, reserved words, parameters, variables, operators, parens) plus the quotePatterns table of quote-style regexes.
src/lexer/regexFactory.ts.inputs: dialect lexical config (keyword lists, IdentChars, QuoteTypes, etc.)
src/lexer/regexFactory.ts.should: Encode SQL quoting/identifier/keyword matching rules; sort keywords longest-first.

src/lexer/regexUtil.ts.summary: Low-level regex string helpers: escapeRegExp, patternToRegex, case-insensitive/dash/prefix pattern builders, and the WHITESPACE_REGEX constant.
src/lexer/regexUtil.ts.should: Provide reusable regex-building primitives for regexFactory.

src/lexer/token.ts.summary: Defines the TokenType enum, Token interface, EOF token helpers, and predicates (testToken, isToken table, isReserved, isLogicalOperator) used across lexer and formatter.
src/lexer/token.ts.should: Enumerate token categories and classify tokens.

src/lexer/NestedComment.ts.summary: NestedComment class implementing the RegExpLike interface to match balanced, nestable /* ... */ block comments that a plain regex cannot.
src/lexer/NestedComment.ts.should: Track nesting depth of /* and */ to find the full comment span.
src/lexer/NestedComment.ts.side: mutates its own lastIndex during matching

src/parser/ast.ts.summary: Type-only module declaring the NodeType enum and every AST node interface (statement, clause, function_call, parenthesis, CASE, limit, literal, identifier, keyword, comments, etc.) plus the AstNode union.
src/parser/ast.ts.should: Define the parse-tree node shapes consumed by the formatter.

src/parser/createParser.ts.summary: createParser() wires a dialect Tokenizer through disambiguateTokens and a LexerAdapter into a Nearley parser over the compiled grammar, returning statements or throwing on invalid/ambiguous SQL.
src/parser/createParser.ts.should: Feed tokens to Nearley and unwrap the single valid result.
src/parser/createParser.ts.shouldNot: Format output or define grammar rules itself.
src/parser/createParser.ts.side: shares mutable paramTypesOverrides with the tokenizer closure

src/parser/LexerAdapter.ts.summary: LexerAdapter bridges the project's Tokenizer to the Nearley lexer interface (reset/next/save/formatError/has), yielding tokens one at a time.
src/parser/LexerAdapter.ts.inputs: a tokenize(chunk)->Token[] function; Nearley lexer calls
src/parser/LexerAdapter.ts.outputs: tokens for Nearley; formatted error strings
src/parser/LexerAdapter.ts.should: Adapt token stream and expose token-type membership to Nearley.
src/parser/LexerAdapter.ts.side: mutates internal index/tokens/input across next() calls

src/languages/sql/sql.formatter.ts.summary: Builds and exports the sql DialectOptions for the base/standard SQL dialect (no dialect-specific postProcess): assembles reserved clause/select/join/set-operation/keyword-phrase lists via expandPhrases and wires tokenizer + format options.
src/languages/sql/sql.formatter.ts.should: Declare this dialect's reserved words, quoting, operators, params, and oneline clauses.
src/languages/sql/sql.formatter.ts.shouldNot: Tokenize, parse, or format directly (only supplies config; any postProcess only retypes tokens).

src/languages/sql/sql.functions.ts.summary: Exports the `functions` string array of built-in sql function names used as reserved-function-name keywords by the tokenizer.
src/languages/sql/sql.functions.ts.should: List this dialect's function names as data.
src/languages/sql/sql.functions.ts.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or non-function keywords.

src/languages/sql/sql.keywords.ts.summary: Exports the `keywords` and `dataTypes` string arrays of reserved sql keywords and data-type names consumed by the tokenizer.
src/languages/sql/sql.keywords.ts.should: List this dialect's reserved keywords and data types as data.
src/languages/sql/sql.keywords.ts.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or function names.

src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.formatte.summary: Builds and exports the bigquery DialectOptions for BigQuery, with its own postProcess that detects array OFFSET subscripts and combines STRUCT/ARRAY<...> parameterized types: assembles reserved clause/select/join/set-operation/keyword-phrase lists via expandPhrases and wires tokenizer + format options.
src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.formatte.should: Declare this dialect's reserved words, quoting, operators, params, and oneline clauses.
src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.formatte.shouldNot: Tokenize, parse, or format directly (only supplies config; any postProcess only retypes tokens).

src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.function.summary: Exports the `functions` string array of built-in bigquery function names used as reserved-function-name keywords by the tokenizer.
src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.function.should: List this dialect's function names as data.
src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.function.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or non-function keywords.

src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.keywords.should: List this dialect's reserved keywords and data types as data.
src/languages/bigquery/bigquery.keywords.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or function names.

src/languages/clickhouse/clickhouse.form.should: Declare this dialect's reserved words, quoting, operators, params, and oneline clauses.
src/languages/clickhouse/clickhouse.form.shouldNot: Tokenize, parse, or format directly (only supplies config; any postProcess only retypes tokens).

src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.format.should: Declare this dialect's reserved words, quoting, operators, params, and oneline clauses.
src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.format.shouldNot: Tokenize, parse, or format directly (only supplies config; any postProcess only retypes tokens).

src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.functi.summary: Exports the `functions` string array of built-in snowflake function names used as reserved-function-name keywords by the tokenizer.
src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.functi.should: List this dialect's function names as data.
src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.functi.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or non-function keywords.

src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.keywor.summary: Exports the `keywords` and `dataTypes` string arrays of reserved snowflake keywords and data-type names consumed by the tokenizer.
src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.keywor.should: List this dialect's reserved keywords and data types as data.
src/languages/snowflake/snowflake.keywor.shouldNot: Contain logic, formatting, or function names.

src/languages/mariadb/likeMariaDb.ts.summary: Shared MariaDB-family postProcess() reused by mariadb/mysql/tidb/singlestoredb: retypes SET before '(' and VALUES after '=' as function-name tokens.
